steps.title: Identify what drives adoption
steps.content: The researchers found that performance expectancy—the perceived benefit of the service—positively influences the intention to adopt premium versions. This is the key signal: perceived benefit directly increases adoption intention.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D reasons that companies should enhance how beneficial users perceive the premium service.

The other choices either overstate the research, exceed what was measured, or contradict the measurement's validity.

This follows directly from the research: if perceived benefit drives adoption, manipulating that perception drives adoption.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
